name: Ardnacrusha
content:
Ardnacrusha is a power station that is not actually in Limerick, but you can walk to it from Limerick. However, I would definitely not recommend walking there. I walked there by accident after I repeatedly got distracted on a Saturday morning stroll that lasted over eight hours. Lots of sheep though.
Ardnacrusha itself is a pretty cool spot. It was the first public infrastructure project the Republic of Ireland undertook after independence, and represented a fifth of the country's GDP at the time. Some parts of the control room design were copied over into the Hoover Dam in the U.S., and if you ever visit both Ardnacrusha and the Hoover Dam, you’ll see the similarities.
